question: On db02, an administrator is working during a cloud VM migration. Which action best fits the Linux+ objective? Scenario SYS-001.
choices: Disable logging first so the system has fewer files to process
Make every affected file world-writable to eliminate permission issues
Reinstall the operating system before collecting command output
Use lsblk and blkid to identify block devices, partitions, filesystems, and UUIDs before editing mounts
explanation: lsblk and blkid are safe discovery commands for storage layout and filesystem identifiers. They should be used before editing /etc/fstab so the administrator does not mount the wrong device.

question: On jumpbox, an administrator is working while preparing a change ticket. Which action best fits the Linux+ objective? Scenario SYS-002.
choices: Make every affected file world-writable to eliminate permission issues
Reinstall the operating system before collecting command output
Create an LVM logical volume with lvcreate, format it, create a mount point, and add a persistent /etc/fstab entry
Disable logging first so the system has fewer files to process
explanation: LVM storage becomes usable only after the logical volume exists, has a filesystem, is mounted, and is made persistent if it must survive reboot.

question: Which TWO actions are appropriate on build01 while onboarding a junior Linux administrator for Linux+ case LP-004?
choices: Delete the relevant configuration file before checking documentation or logs
Use modprobe to load a kernel module and lsmod or modinfo to verify module state and metadata
Skip evidence collection and reboot repeatedly until the symptom disappears
Inspect /proc, /sys, and command output before changing kernel or hardware-related settings
Grant full root access to every user so administration tasks are easier
explanation: modprobe handles module dependencies when loading kernel modules. lsmod shows loaded modules and modinfo displays module metadata. Also, /proc and /sys expose runtime kernel and device information. They help administrators verify current state before making persistent changes.
